name: =?utf-8?Q?The=20White=20House?=
subject: President Trump takes a stand for veterans
body:
President Donald J. Trump believes in standing up for America’s veterans—both during our National Anthem and through the care...

https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=91645a4fff&e=3ee4985eea
The White House • November 15, 2018

** President Trump takes a stand for veterans
------------------------------------------------------------
President Donald J. Trump believes in standing up for America’s veterans—both during our National Anthem and through the care and support our Government provides them with each and every day.

“Together, we will honor those who defend us. We will cherish those who protect us. And we will celebrate the amazing heroes who keep America safe and strong and proud and free,” President Trump said today (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=0d33dfa9c2&e=3ee4985eea) . “To every veteran and military family across our land, I want to express the eternal gratitude and thanks of our entire Nation.”

Earlier today, the President and First Lady Melania Trump visited the Marine Barracks in Washington, D.C., thanking service members and providing them with desserts in honor of the Thanksgiving holiday next week. The First Couple also spent time with the Marines who bravely responded to a building fire at the Arthur Senior Public Housing complex in Washington on September 19.

Later in the afternoon, President Trump hosted veterans and military families at the White House in celebration of National Veterans and Military Families Month. He took time during the event to explain his commitment to our country’s heroes:
* In June, the President signed the VA MISSION Act, which unleashes the largest reform of the Veterans Affairs system in half a century.

* Since Inauguration Day, the Trump Administration has prioritized accountability at the VA, removing more than 3,600 Government employees who weren't treating our veterans with the respect and care they deserve.

* Programs at the VA and the Department of Housing and Urban Development have helped nearly 54,000 veterans find permanent housing—and thanks to a booming economy, veterans’ unemployment is near a two-decade low.

How President Trump is honoring the sacrifices of America’s veterans (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=cdf058ac3d&e=3ee4985eea)

Watch: The President and First Lady visit the Marine Barracks (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=e27e9befc0&e=3ee4985eea)

** The turkeys are coming!
------------------------------------------------------------
Next week, President Trump will continue the time-honored White House tradition of officially pardoning the National Thanksgiving Turkey. Before he does, Americans will vote on which turkey he pardons.

Be the first to know when voting begins! (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=39332c4436&e=3ee4985eea)

** Photo of the Day
------------------------------------------------------------
Official White House Photo by Andrea Hanks
President Donald J. Trump and First Lady Melania Trump visit with service members at the Marine Barracks in Washington, D.C. | November 15, 2018

Privacy Policy (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=80cce9ad59&e=3ee4985eea) | Contact the White House (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8e9f6997fe&e=3ee4985eea) | Unsubscribe (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8cafb01033&e=3ee4985eea&c=9dd57767db)

============================================================
The White House . 1600 Pennsylvania Ave NW . Washington, DC 20500 . USA · 202-456-1111

name: Andrew Gillum
subject: Count Every Vote
body:
[](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb35c2/9e00690/69e77b98/2400a11c/432096510/VEsH/ "https://andrewgillum.com/")
Friends,

Our work in Florida hasn't stopped yet -- because a vote denied is justice denied. While our race has fallen outside the window for a manual recount, we still believe that before the election results are certified, the State of Florida must count every legally cast vote.

**We know that there are tens of thousands of votes that have yet to be counted -- and we plan to do all we can to ensure that every voice is heard in this process.
**
[](www.andrewgillum.com/vote)
---

Voters need to know that their decision to participate in this election, and every election, matters. Laws that restrict our fellow Floridians from voting -- from signature matching, to requiring vote-by-mail ballots arrive before polls close -- need to take their rightful place alongside the literacy tests, poll taxes, and grandfather clauses of days past.

If you voted-by-mail or cast a provisional ballot and your ballot was disqualified because of your signature, you need to contact your Supervisor of Elections by Saturday at 5 pm. Every voice needs to be heard.

**[If you need to contact your Supervisor of Elections to make sure your ballot was counted, make sure you visit www.andrewgillum.com/vote to find out more information.](www.andrewgillum.com/vote)**

Our election will conclude when the State of Florida has decided to count every vote -- and I will continue to speak up and speak out for each and every one of you.

Respectfully,

Andrew Gillum

Paid by Andrew Gillum, Democrat, for Governor.

---
Andrew Gillum for Governor
240 N. Magnolia Drive
Tallahassee, FL 32301

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is one of the most important tools we have to reach supporters like you, but you can let us know if you'd like to [receive fewer emails](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb35c2/9e00690/69e77b98/2400a11f/432096510/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IiJ9/ "http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/s/receive-fewer-emails?source=email_footer&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip="). We'd hate to see you go, but if you need to do so, click here to [unsubscribe](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb35c2/9e00690/69e77b98/2400a11e/432096510/VEsF/ "https://gillum.cp.bsd.net/page/unsubscribe/").

name: =?utf-8?Q?The=20White=20House?=
subject: It’s the right time to pass criminal justice reform
body:
With President Trump leading the way, the push is on to get serious criminal justice reform done by Christmas. | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=7367401544&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| | |
# It’s the Right Time to Pass Criminal Justice Reform

“With President Trump leading the way, the push is on to get serious criminal justice reform done by Christmas. Resisters on the left and right would be fools to stand in the way,” the _New York Post_ editorial board writes.

Earlier this week, President Trump called on Congress to pass the FIRST STEP Act, which would fund educational and vocational training programs in U.S. prisons while easing certain mandatory-minimum sentencing rules. “Trump has it right: “We’re all better off when former inmates can receive [a second chance] and re-enter society as law-abiding, productive citizens,” the _Post’s_ editors write.

[Click here to read more.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=4eddec840c&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| “President Trump will award the Presidential Medal of Freedom on Friday to seven recipients, including a trio of former professional athletes,” Tom Joyce reports for the _[Washington Examiner](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=5e24c52714&e=3ee4985eea)_. The Medal of Freedom is awarded to “individuals who have made especially meritorious contributions to the security or national interests of the United States, to world peace, or to cultural or other significant public or private endeavors.” Joyce’s view: “It is fair to say this year’s recipients have done just that.”
---
|
---
| A stronger Middle East “is an American national interest, and the Trump administration would like our friends to be working together on security and prosperity for the region, including Israel and its neighbors,” White House Special Representative for International Negotiations Jason Greenblatt writes in _[The Hill](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=b3da50ea99&e=3ee4985eea)_. “All of these efforts are not meant to leave the Palestinians behind; on the contrary, they will help pave the path to a possible peace agreement between the Israelis and Palestinians.”
---
|
---
| “President Trump is expected to travel to California over the weekend amid the wildfires that have ravaged both ends of the state,” Elizabeth Zwirz reports for [Fox News](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=6dbe8de6a6&e=3ee4985eea). “I just approved an expedited request for a Major Disaster Declaration for the State of California,” the President tweeted early this week. “Wanted to respond quickly in order to alleviate some of the incredible suffering going on. I am with you all the way. God Bless all of the victims and families affected.”
---
|
---
| In the _[Washington Examiner](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=04a34d4439&e=3ee4985eea)_ , Anna Giaritelli reports that the Trump Administration has given the green light for $324 million to replace 32 miles of existing border wall in Arizona. “The replacement wall is meant to keep pedestrians and vehicles from entering the U.S. Older barriers were largely to prevent cars from driving over, but were only a few feet tall and did little to keep people from trespassing from Mexico.”
---
|

[Privacy Policy](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=a9c8de677b&e=3ee4985eea) | [Contact the White House](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=915c34a069&e=3ee4985eea) | [Unsubscribe](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8cafb01033&e=3ee4985eea&c=afa0736d31)
---
| | | The White House * 1600 Pennsylvania Ave NW * Washington, DC 20500 * USA · 202-456-1111
---

name: FL HAND RECOUNT
subject: The fight continues →
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Florida Democratic Party
body:
|
---
| **" FLORIDA SENATE RACE HEADS TO A HAND RECOUNT" **
--The Hill, 11/15/2018

The fight to ensure every valid vote is counted continues, team!

**The recount for Florida 's Senate race must be completed by this Sunday.**

The more lawyers and staff we can have on the ground through Sunday's deadline, the better.

**That 's why we need to raise another $150,000 for our Recount Fund to keep up our work fighting for Floridians' votes.**

name: Sherrod Brown
subject: Mike can win in Mississippi runoff
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Sherrod Brown
body:
|
---

| | **" Republicans who are paying close attention ... are now extremely concerned about where the state of play is in this race"**
\-- Mississippi-based GOP consultant in _The Hill_ ; 11/15/2018 If Republicans are getting nervous, we should be getting excited about Mike's chances to win the runoff election for U.S. Senate in Mississippi. He's got the drive. He's got the ground team in place. **Now all he needs is the money.** [**Peter, will you rush in $5 or more to Mike Espy's Senate campaign in Mississippi?**](https://secure.sherrodbrown.com/page/m/302ffd65/4462dc26/8b0b8844/201f05c1/1675900819/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZEVDA1VEpTVT0iOiIzMjY4MTgzIiwiZTN0emJIVm5mWDA9Ijoic2JlMTgxMTE2IiwiZTN0MlpYSnphVzl1ZlgwPSI6Im0ifQ==/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/sbe181116?refcode=sbe181116_m_other&refcode2=3268183") _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will be evenly split between Mike Espy and Sherrod Brown:_ **[Express Donate: $5](https://secure.sherrodbrown.com/page/m/302ffd65/4462dc26/8b0b8844/201f05c0/1675900819/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZEVDA1VEpTVT0iOiIzMjY4MTgzIiwiZTN0emJIVm5mWDA9Ijoic2JlMTgxMTE2IiwiZTN0aGJXOTFiblI5ZlE9PSI6IjUiLCJlM3QyWlhKemFXOXVmWDA9IjoibSJ9/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/sbe181116?express_lane=true&amount=5&refcode=sbe181116_m_5&refcode2=3268183")** **[Express Donate: $10](https://secure.sherrodbrown.com/page/m/302ffd65/4462dc26/8b0b8844/201f05c0/1675900819/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZEVDA1VEpTVT0iOiIzMjY4MTgzIiwiZTN0emJIVm5mWDA9Ijoic2JlMTgxMTE2IiwiZTN0aGJXOTFiblI5ZlE9PSI6IjEwIiwiZTN0MlpYSnphVzl1ZlgwPSI6Im0ifQ==/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/sbe181116?express_lane=true&amount=10&refcode=sbe181116_m_10&refcode2=3268183")** **[Express Donate: $26](https://secure.sherrodbrown.com/page/m/302ffd65/4462dc26/8b0b8844/201f05c0/1675900819/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZEVDA1VEpTVT0iOiIzMjY4MTgzIiwiZTN0emJIVm5mWDA9Ijoic2JlMTgxMTE2IiwiZTN0aGJXOTFiblI5ZlE9PSI6IjI2IiwiZTN0MlpYSnphVzl1ZlgwPSI6Im0ifQ==/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/sbe181116?express_lane=true&amount=26&refcode=sbe181116_m_26&refcode2=3268183")** **[Or donate another amount.](https://secure.sherrodbrown.com/page/m/302ffd65/4462dc26/8b0b8844/201f05c1/1675900819/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZEVDA1VEpTVT0iOiIzMjY4MTgzIiwiZTN0emJIVm5mWDA9Ijoic2JlMTgxMTE2IiwiZTN0MlpYSnphVzl1ZlgwPSI6Im0ifQ==/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/sbe181116?refcode=sbe181116_m_other&refcode2=3268183")** I'll tell you what I'm excited about: I'm excited that Mike could be the first African-American elected to the U.S. Senate from Mississippi in well over a century. I'm excited at the possibility of having another U.S. Senator who understands the dignity of work and will fight for policies that put workers first. **I'm excited that we can make a real difference for Mike by helping him fund his runoff effort.** Of course, whether we do or not is up to you Peter. [**Let's make Mike Espy the next U.S. Senator from Mississippi. Chip in $5 or more.**](https://secure.sherrodbrown.com/page/m/302ffd65/4462dc26/8b0b8844/201f05c1/1675900819/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZEVDA1VEpTVT0iOiIzMjY4MTgzIiwiZTN0emJIVm5mWDA9Ijoic2JlMTgxMTE2IiwiZTN0MlpYSnphVzl1ZlgwPSI6Im0ifQ==/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/sbe181116?refcode=sbe181116_m_other&refcode2=3268183") We have one of the strongest grassroots teams in the country -- that's how we won in Ohio. Let's get to work for Mike. clean_body: | | | " Republicans who are paying close attention ... are now extremely concerned about where the state of play is in this race" \-- Mississippi-based GOP consultant in The Hill ; 11/15/2018 If Republicans are getting nervous, we should be getting excited about Mike's chances to win the runoff election for U.S. Senate in Mississippi. He's got the drive. He's got the ground team in place. Now all he needs is the money. "" If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will be evenly split between Mike Espy and Sherrod Brown: "" "" "" "" I'll tell you what I'm excited about: I'm excited that Mike could be the first African-American elected to the U.S. Senate from Mississippi in well over a century. I'm excited at the possibility of having another U.S. Senator who understands the dignity of work and will fight for policies that put workers first. I'm excited that we can make a real difference for Mike by helping him fund his runoff effort. Of course, whether we do or not is up to you Peter. "" We have one of the strongest grassroots teams in the country -- that's how we won in Ohio. Let's get to work for Mike. With gratitude, Sherrod | | | "" | "" | | Paid for by Friends of Sherrod Brown Contributions or gifts to Friends of Sherrod Brown are not tax deductible.

name: Team O'Halleran
subject: tell Tom what you think!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Tom O'Halleran for Congress
body:
Now that Democrats have taken back the House majority, we have a historic opportunity to deliver results for Arizona families. And Tom knows that good public servants listen to their constituents befo
Tom O'Halleran is running for Congress in Arizona's 1st district, a critical race to getting Congress back on track. Click here to donate to Tom's campaign and help him put Arizona families first.
[](http://action.tomohalleran.com/page/m/1d633d9a/1a8421e1/19b80e23a/4980ef2a/3557882227/VEsH/ "http://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/ohalleranq32018?refcode=em_header&recurring=auto&amount=5&amounts=15,25,50,100,250,500,1000")
---
Friends, Here at Team O'Halleran, we're overwhelmed with gratitude that Arizona voters chose to re-elect Tom last week. Tom is the independent voice we deserve standing up for us in Congress, and he is so excited to continue serving us in our nation's capital. And now that Democrats have taken back the House majority, we have a historic opportunity to deliver results for Arizona families -- _so we 're hard at work planning for the busy two years ahead!_ Tom knows that good public servants listen to their constituents before setting their priorities. [**So, friends, will you take our Priorities Survey, and tell us what you 'd like the next Congress to deliver?**](http://action.tomohalleran.com/page/m/1d633d9a/1a8421e1/19b80e23a/4980ef2b/3557882227/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZGYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVkdhWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIiwiSlNWTVlYTjBibUZ0WlNVbCI6IiIsIkpTVmFhWEFsSlE9PSI6Ijg1MzA3In0=/ "http://action.tomohalleran.com/page/s/tell-tom?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&zip=85307") | ****[Tell Tom what you think! Take our survey](http://action.tomohalleran.com/page/m/1d633d9a/1a8421e1/19b80e23a/4980ef2b/3557882227/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZGYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVkdhWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIiwiSlNWTVlYTjBibUZ0WlNVbCI6IiIsIkpTVmFhWEFsSlE9PSI6Ijg1MzA3In0=/ "http://action.tomohalleran.com/page/s/tell-tom?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&zip=85307")****
---

Thank you again for your continued support, friends!

Thanks,

Team O'Halleran

****[DONATE](http://action.tomohalleran. "http://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/ohalleranq42018?refcode=em_pet_2018.11.16_b1_to_all&recurring=auto&amount=10&amounts=10,25,50,100,250,500,1000&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Glendale&state=AZ&zip=85307&phone=&occupation=&employer=")****
---

**_**Representative Tom O 'Halleran represents Arizona's First Congressional District in the 115th Congress. As a former police officer, small businessman, and Arizona state legislator, Tom knows what it takes to bring people together and bring positive change to Arizona families. Tom is taking on the tough challenges in Congress by championing ethics reforms and putting problem solving ahead of politics. If you'd like to support Tom's campaign please [click here](http://action.tomohalleran. "http://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/ohalleranq42018?refcode=footer-bio&recurring=auto&amount=10&amounts=10,25,50,100,250,500,1000&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Glendale&state=AZ&zip=85307&phone=&occupation=&employer=").**_**
** **
** **
** **
** **

Paid for by Tom O'Halleran for Congress

name: Tracy Newman
subject: This is far from the last fight
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jay Inslee for Washington
body:
Let's make sure we can last through the winter.

| [](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd7b000/2f5ea88d/7b606dc7/2cbdb83a/1905267729/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/power-our-progress?utm_source=ema_2018-11-16_pre-freeze-3&refcode=ema_2018-11-16_pre-freeze-3&utm_campaign=pre-freeze-3&utm_medium=ema") Friend, What's happening in the Florida and Georgia governors' races right now is proof that we cannot stop fighting. Republicans are actively suppressing votes that have yet to be counted for Stacey Abrams and Andrew Gillum. Donald Trump is inventing conspiracy theories about illegal votes. While in the states, officials are trying to use law enforcement to stop ballots from being counted or flat-out lying about the number of votes still out there. This isn't about one election or party affiliation -- it's about protecting one of our most sacred freedoms as Americans. This fight could shape the future of our country for generations to come. That's why Jay is already hard at work. He's making plans for how we can be ready to respond whenever Republicans try to play us with one of their schemes. And he's building up our progressive power, so we can keep the nation's attention on issues like this by refusing to let them operate in silence. But we have a major deadline coming up: Our fundraising freeze hits in December. We won't be able to fundraise for our governor's race for months. And yet, we have to be prepared to take on whatever they throw at us. [**So I'm asking you today, one month before our freeze: Will you help make sure we can last through the winter?**](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd7b000/2f5ea88d/7b606dc7/2cbdb83a/1905267729/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/power-our-progress?utm_source=ema_2018-11-16_pre-freeze-3&refcode=ema_2018-11-16_pre-freeze-3&utm_campaign=pre-freeze-3&utm_medium=ema") It's important we have the resources for battles to come. Even though we won the House last week, you and I both know that Trump and his enablers in the Senate will continue promoting extreme policies and fighting to get more life-long appointees. This fight in Florida and Georgia is far from the last time we'll see Republicans try to suppress our votes. Let's make sure Washington can keep setting the example for the nation and that we stay prepared to fight back all winter, no matter what Trump does. [**For the sake of our progressive movement, I'm asking you to step up now. We won't be able to raise any money for the governor's race for months -- and who knows what could happen in that time. We have a pre-freeze deadline today to make sure we can last through the winter. Can you chip in to help us power our progress, no matter what happens next?**](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd7b000/2f5ea88d/7b606dc7/2cbdb83a/1905267729/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/power-our-progress?utm_source=ema_2018-11-16_pre-freeze-3&refcode=ema_2018-11-16_pre-freeze-3&utm_campaign=pre-freeze-3&utm_medium=ema") Thank you, Tracy Newman | [CONTRIBUTE »](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd7b000/2f5ea88d/7b606dc7/2cbdb83a/1905267729/VEsC/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/power-our-progress?utm_source=ema_2018-11-16_pre-freeze-3&refcode=ema_2018-11-16_pre-freeze-3&utm_campaign=pre-freeze-3&utm_medium=ema")
---

_Now more than ever, we must support progressive leadership in America. Chip in to show you're with Jay Inslee as we fight for progress in Washington state and around the country._

| [CONTRIBUTE »](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd7b000/2f5ea88d/7b606dc7/2cbdb83a/1905267729/VEsD/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/power-our-progress?utm_source=ema_2018-11-16_pre-freeze-3&refcode=ema_2018-11-16_pre-freeze-3&utm_campaign=pre-freeze-3&utm_medium=ema")
---

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. [Click here to unsubscribe](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd7b000/2f5ea88d/7b606dc7/2cbdb839/1905267729/VEsA/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/ "https://action.jayinslee.com/unsubscribe?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com"). name: =?utf-8?Q?Indiana=20Republican=20Party?=
subject: Why I'm thankful
body:
| | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/indiana/why-im-thankful?e=1acd9bb3d6)
---
| |
---
| **_Why I'm Thankful_** With Thanksgiving just days away, there's certainly no shortage of things to be thankful for -- both as Hoosiers and as Republicans.

I'm thankful for the trust Hoosiers have placed in us. I'm thankful for our great state's leaders. And I'm thankful for the boundless opportunities still ahead as we continue to make a difference in the lives of all Hoosiers.

Here at your Indiana Republican Party, we're already working toward more victories in 2019 and 2020. But this week, we're also taking time to reflect on all that we have and to give thanks.

Happy Thanksgiving! -Kyle Hupfer
---
|
---
| | | **GOVERNOR HOLCOMB'S WEEK THAT WAS**
---
|
---
---
| **_Growing Indiana's Workforce_** For **Governor Eric Holcomb** , helping Hoosiers build the skills needed to succeed in today’s economy is a top priority – and it was the focus of two events this week!

First, on Thursday,**Governor Holcomb** was in Washington, D.C., to lead the Jobs for America’s Graduates (JAG) board meeting, filling his role as the organization’s national chairman. JAG helps at-risk students stay in school and work toward building the skills for a great career! Indiana is proudly home to one of the largest JAG programs in the nation!

And then earlier today,**Governor Holcomb** helped break ground on tech giant Infosys’ new U.S. Education Center, which will be built on the site of the former Indianapolis International Airport terminal. The global company will use Indiana as a U.S. base for training its workforce to fill the latest in high-tech jobs – in addition to creating 3,000 new jobs of its own right here in Indiana.

Thank you,**Governor Holcomb** , for your dedication to helping all Hoosiers do what it takes to fill high-wage, high-skill jobs!
---
|
---
| | **CONGRATULATIONS, SENATOR YOUNG!**
---
|
---
---
| **_Senator Todd Young Elected NRSC Chairman!_** Indiana’s soon-to-be senior United States Senator, **Todd Young** , had a big week. He was just elected by his colleagues as the new chairman of the National Republican Senatorial Committee (NRSC) for the 2020 general election cycle!

It’s a big job, as he’ll be responsible for leading the NRSC as it works to protect the U.S. Senate for Republicans. This will include protecting 22 Republican-held seats and working to pick up 12 Democrat-held seats. But, as we saw with his resounding victory in 2016 and his work to help elect Mike Braun this year, **Senator Young** knows how to win tough races – and that's exactly what he'll do again in 2020!
---
|
---
| | **MIKE BRAUN CORNER**
---
|
---
---
| **_Welcome to Washington, Mike Braun!_** This week, **Mike Braun** went to Washington! As our new senator-elect, he’s gotten straight to business, opening an office on Capitol Hill and taking meetings with U.S. Senate leaders like Senate Majority Leader Mitch McConnell. Keep an eye out for him – he’s about to do big things!
---
|
---
| _**Upcoming Republican Events**_ [December 1](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=0d6dfae026&e=1acd9bb3d6): Kosciusko County Republican Women's Christmas Party
[December 3](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=748f2d46db&e=1acd9bb3d6): Greater Indianapolis Republican Women's Club Annual Holiday Luncheon
[December 8](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=ec7e011885&e=1acd9bb3d6): Wells County Republican Christmas Open House
[April 6](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=5126b66df2&e=1acd9bb3d6): Decatur County Lincoln Day Dinner
[April 13](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=7f2890272c&e=1acd9bb3d6): Vermillion County Lincoln Day Dinner
---
|
---
| _**News You Can Use**_ **Governor Eric Holcomb**[issues proclamation declaring November 15th "America Recycles Day"](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=84a6662db2&e=1acd9bb3d6)
**Lt. Governor Suzanne Crouch**[speaks at the 2nd Annual Women in Business Conference in Greenfield](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=d63a28c526&e=1acd9bb3d6)
**Secretary of State Connie Lawson **[calls on White House to reverse Obama-era election polic](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=71fef81613&e=1acd9bb3d6)[y](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=6a26dfae6b&e=1acd9bb3d6)
**Treasurer Kelly Mitchell**[thanks American heroes on Veteran's Day](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=2a1185cb7d&e=1acd9bb3d6)
**Auditor Tera Klutz **[is excited to continue serving as Indiana's first elected CPA auditor](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=16f8b71604&e=1acd9bb3d6)
**Superintendent Jennifer McCormick** [says school safety focus is "broad"](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=51e3e66235&e=1acd9bb3d6)
**Senator Todd Young **[celebrates National Apprenticeship Week with Secretary of Labor Alexander Acosta](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=7c4600822c&e=1acd9bb3d6)
**Congresswoman Jackie Walorski**[discusses how to strengthen the Hoosier workforce](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=476cb6c4f3&e=1acd9bb3d6)
**Congressman Jim Banks** [stands with Nikki Haley's support of Israel in vote on UN resolution](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=5a4794f4e8&e=1acd9bb3d6)
**Congressman Todd Rokita **[congratulates Rep. Kevin McCarthy on being elected House Minority Leader](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=d33397a9b9&e=1acd9bb3d6)
**Congresswoman Susan Brooks**[helps introduce stomach cancer resolution to honor Matthew Tully](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=ace15fc30c&e=1acd9bb3d6)
**Congressman Larry Bucshon** [remembers and thanks all veterans who answered the call to service](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=d6e8e85e81&e=1acd9bb3d6)
**Congressman Trey Hollingsworth**[remembers the lives lost 100 years ago in World War I](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=0f078d5250&e=1acd9bb3d6)

clean_body: | | | | | | Why I'm Thankful With Thanksgiving just days away, there's certainly no shortage of things to be thankful for -- both as Hoosiers and as Republicans. I'm thankful for the trust Hoosiers have placed in us. I'm thankful for our great state's leaders. And I'm thankful for the boundless opportunities still ahead as we continue to make a difference in the lives of all Hoosiers. Here at your Indiana Republican Party, we're already working toward more victories in 2019 and 2020. But this week, we're also taking time to reflect on all that we have and to give thanks. Happy Thanksgiving! -Kyle Hupfer | | | | GOVERNOR HOLCOMB'S WEEK THAT WAS | | Growing Indiana's Workforce For Governor Eric Holcomb , helping Hoosiers build the skills needed to succeed in today’s economy is a top priority – and it was the focus of two events this week! First, on Thursday,Governor Holcomb was in Washington, D.C., to lead the Jobs for America’s Graduates (JAG) board meeting, filling his role as the organization’s national chairman. JAG helps at-risk students stay in school and work toward building the skills for a great career! Indiana is proudly home to one of the largest JAG programs in the nation! And then earlier today,Governor Holcomb helped break ground on tech giant Infosys’ new U.S. Education Center, which will be built on the site of the former Indianapolis International Airport terminal. The global company will use Indiana as a U.S. base for training its workforce to fill the latest in high-tech jobs – in addition to creating 3,000 new jobs of its own right here in Indiana. Thank you,Governor Holcomb , for your dedication to helping all Hoosiers do what it takes to fill high-wage, high-skill jobs! | | | CONGRATULATIONS, SENATOR YOUNG! | | Senator Todd Young Elected NRSC Chairman! Indiana’s soon-to-be senior United States Senator, Todd Young , had a big week. He was just elected by his colleagues as the new chairman of the National Republican Senatorial Committee (NRSC) for the 2020 general election cycle! It’s a big job, as he’ll be responsible for leading the NRSC as it works to protect the U.S. Senate for Republicans. This will include protecting 22 Republican-held seats and working to pick up 12 Democrat-held seats. But, as we saw with his resounding victory in 2016 and his work to help elect Mike Braun this year, Senator Young knows how to win tough races – and that's exactly what he'll do again in 2020! | | | MIKE BRAUN CORNER | | Welcome to Washington, Mike Braun! This week, Mike Braun went to Washington! As our new senator-elect, he’s gotten straight to business, opening an office on Capitol Hill and taking meetings with U.S. Senate leaders like Senate Majority Leader Mitch McConnell. Keep an eye out for him – he’s about to do big things! | | Upcoming Republican Events : Kosciusko County Republican Women's Christmas Party : Greater Indianapolis Republican Women's Club Annual Holiday Luncheon : Wells County Republican Christmas Open House : Decatur County Lincoln Day Dinner : Vermillion County Lincoln Day Dinner | | News You Can Use Governor Eric Holcomb Lt.
